title: Marinara RPG Ruleset
description: Declarative spec for a tabletop RPG ruleset that the Marinara-RPG-RP-Mode-Extension overlay applies to Marinara Engine's Roleplay Mode. The overlay reads this file to render the character sheet, format dice tags, instruct the overlay agent, and validate skill resolution. One ruleset per Roleplay Mode chat.

id.description: Unique kebab-case identifier (e.g. dnd5e, exalted3e, pbta-monster-of-the-week). Used as the localStorage key for ruleset selection.

name.description: Human-readable ruleset name shown in the UI selector.

version.description: SemVer of this ruleset definition file (not the underlying RPG's edition).

edition.description: The RPG edition this ruleset models, for human reference (e.g. "5e (SRD 5.1)", "Exalted 3rd Edition Core (2016)").

license.description: License string for this ruleset definition file's content (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0", "Original mechanics references; charm names belong to Onyx Path").

summary.description: One-paragraph description of the ruleset's flavor, suitable for the README ruleset list.

commitmentModel.description: Optional declaration of how items in this ruleset are bound to a character to grant their magical / mechanical effects. "attuned" — D&D 5e style: each item has an Attuned boolean; cap of 3 attuned items at once. "invested" — Pathfinder 2e style: each item has an Invested boolean; cap of 10 invested items at once. "mote" — Exalted style: each item has a Mote Commitment integer plus a Personal/Peripheral pool selection; commitment is subtracted from the chosen pool's current value while the item is committed, and restored when uncommitted. null (default) — ruleset has no commitment mechanic; the inventory editor hides the commitment section.

xpTable.description: Optional ordered array of {level, xp} entries for formula-based XP progression (D&D 5e, PF2e). The XP card uses this to render the next-level threshold and progress bar. Pool-based rulesets (Exalted) omit this; their XP card shows current/total + a +1 button instead.

dice.description: Primary die used for resolution and a canonical notation example.

type.description: Primary die size. dF means Fudge/FATE dice.

notation.description: Example notation the GM should use in [dice: ...] tags (e.g. "1d20+MOD", "Xd10 vs 7").

resolution.description: How a check is resolved. Different modes have different required sub-fields.

modifierFormula.description: Plain-language formula the GM applies (e.g. "1d20 + ability_mod + proficiency_bonus").

skillBonusFormula.description: Optional auto-calculated skill (and save) bonus formula for d20-style systems. When set, the sheet replaces the per-skill stepper with the computed bonus. Tokens: {linkedAttribute_mod} (the skill's linked attribute modifier), {tierBonus} (the active proficiency tier's rollBonusFormula evaluated), and any {StatName}. D&D 5e: "{linkedAttribute_mod} + {tierBonus}". PF2e: "{Level} + {linkedAttribute_mod} + {tierBonus}".

spellSaveDcFormula.description: Optional spell save DC formula for d20-style systems. When set, the sheet's Cast button computes the DC and posts it in the [mrrp-cast: dc=...] chat tag so the GM agent resolves saves. Token: {spellcastingAttribute_mod} resolves to the casting spell's declared attribute modifier. When omitted, the cast tag posts without a DC and the agent picks one by rule context — the core extension knows no system's DC convention by default. Examples: D&D 5e "8 + {Proficiency Bonus} + {spellcastingAttribute_mod}"; PF2e "10 + {Level} + {spellcastingAttribute_mod}".

attackProficiencyFormula.description: Optional formula for the proficiency contribution to weapon attack rolls when an item is marked attackProficient. When set, the sheet's `atk` button evaluates this formula and pre-fills the dice widget's Proficiency input. When omitted, attacks contribute zero proficiency and the player can type a value manually. The core extension knows no system's proficiency convention by default. Examples: D&D 5e "{Proficiency Bonus}"; PF2e (trained tier) "{Level} + 2".

poolFormula.description: Plain-language formula for pool size (e.g. "Attribute + Ability").

target.description: Face value at which a die counts as a success (Exalted: 7).

doubles.description: Rule for dice that count as more than one success.

trigger.description: How a botch is detected: any 1 with no successes, more 1s than successes, or any roll of the face.

diceFormula.description: Standard XdY dice notation for the roll that is summed and compared against the target. Percentile roll-under (Call of Cthulhu, BRP, Runequest, Pendragon) uses "1d100"; stat-vs-XdY roll-under (GURPS, traditional BRP) uses "3d6". The dice are summed and the total must be <= target for success.

skillBonusFormula.description: Optional formula that resolves a check's TARGET from the skill row's stored value and other stats. Tokens: {skill} resolves to the skill's stored value, {StatName} resolves to attribute/derived values, {Level} resolves to character level. Defaults to "{skill}" when omitted (raw skill value = target, the BRP/CoC convention).

criticalSuccessFormula.description: Optional formula evaluated against the resolved target. When the rolled total is <= this evaluated number, the result is flagged as a critical success. Token {target} resolves to the effective target value. Examples: Call of Cthulhu 7e "{target} / 5"; GURPS literal constant "4". When omitted, no critical success band applies.

criticalFailureThreshold.description: Optional integer threshold. When the rolled total is >= this value, the result is flagged as a fumble. Call of Cthulhu 7e: 96 (rolls of 96-100 fumble). Cannot be combined with criticalFailureFormula. When neither field is set, no fumble band applies.

criticalFailureFormula.description: Optional formula alternative to criticalFailureThreshold. Evaluated against the resolved target; when the rolled total is >= the evaluated number the result is flagged as a fumble. Token {target} resolves to the effective target value, {margin} resolves to (target - total). Used by GURPS where fumble depends on margin. When neither this field nor criticalFailureThreshold is set, no fumble band applies.

modifierFormula.description: Plain-language formula for the +modifier (e.g. "Skill rating" or "Approach rating"). The roll is always 4dF + this modifier.

ladder.description: Fate ladder mapping integer ratings to verbal labels (Mediocre 0, Average 1, ... Legendary 8). Used as display data for the GM agent's narration vocabulary and the sheet renderer; the dice math operates on raw integers.

successWithStyle.description: The minimum positive margin (rolled total - target) that counts as 'success with style' (Fate Core: 3).

diceType.description: Face count of each pool die (Lasers & Feelings uses d6). Each die is compared independently to stat; successes are counted per the active stance's direction.

poolFormula.description: Plain-language formula for pool size (e.g. "1 + (prepared ? 1 : 0) + (expert ? 1 : 0) + assistance"). The dice widget uses this string as the Pool input's label; the player types the resolved integer pool size each roll.

stat.description: Name of the single character attribute that serves as the per-die threshold (L&F: "Number").

stances.description: Exactly two stance options. Each declares the success direction relative to stat. One stance must use direction=under and the other direction=over — the schema enforces this pairing via `allOf` + `contains`/`minContains` so two same-direction stances fail validation rather than relying on the dice widget's soft-failure check.

id.description: Stable identifier (kebab-case). Used in chat tags (e.g. stance=lasers).

label.description: Display label rendered on the stance toggle (e.g. "LASERS", "FEELINGS").

direction.description: Strict comparison: "under" means face<stat counts as a success; "over" means face>stat counts as a success. Equality is handled separately by exactMatch.

description.description: Optional in-fiction guidance shown in tooltip / GM agent prompt.

exactMatch.description: Optional rule for dice that roll exactly equal to stat. With strict under/over comparisons on stances, exact-match dice never count via the stance direction — this block controls whether they also count as successes plus any narration hook.

name.description: Display name for the exact-match outcome (L&F: "LASER FEELINGS").

countsAsSuccess.description: When true, each exact-match die adds 1 to the success total. L&F: true.

effect.description: Plain-language description of the in-fiction effect (L&F: "Ask the GM a question and they answer honestly").

narrationHook.description: Token the GM agent matches against in the chat tag (e.g. "laser_feelings") to know it owes a special beat.

outcomeTiers.description: Ordered tiers from worst to best. Each tier is reached when total successes >= minSuccesses; if maxSuccesses is set, the tier is bounded above too (otherwise it is the open-ended top tier). The dice widget walks the array and picks the LAST tier whose minSuccesses <= total successes.

minSuccesses.description: Inclusive lower bound of total successes for this tier.

maxSuccesses.description: Optional inclusive upper bound. Omit on the open-ended top tier.

label.description: Short outcome label (L&F: miss / barely / good / critical).

narration.description: Default narration the GM agent can riff on for this tier.

poolFormula.description: Plain-language formula for pool size in DICE (e.g. "Attribute + Skill" for OpenD6, where attribute and skill values are stored as integer dice counts). The dice widget rolls this many `dieSize`-siders, sums their face values, adds the resolved pips (see `pipsField`), and compares to the difficulty entered at roll time. Sister mode to `dice-pool`: dice-pool counts dice meeting a per-die target (Storyteller systems); dice-pool-sum totals face values vs a difficulty number (D6 family — OpenD6, WEG Star Wars, Mini Six, D6 System SRD, MEGS).

dieSize.description: Face count of each pool die. D6-family systems: 6. Documented enum keeps the dice-widget parser tight.

pipsField.description: Optional name of the sheet field that carries +pips (the +P in OpenD6's NDX+P notation, where pips are 1/3-of-a-die granularity). The widget adds this resolved integer to the rolled sum before comparing to difficulty. Pair with each attribute/skill's `pipGranularity` block (added in v0.2.2). When omitted, no flat-pip bonus is added.

difficultyHint.description: Recommended default difficulty number the dice widget shows as the difficulty input's placeholder. Player or GM types a different number per roll. OpenD6 Adventure: 15 (Easy 10, Difficult 20, Heroic 30). v0.2.2 limits this to a static integer; opposed-roll formulas deferred to a later release.

wildDie.description: Optional Wild Die mechanic — one die in every pool gets special handling. D6-family canon (OpenD6, WEG Star Wars, Mini Six): explodeFace=6 cascades upward; critFailFace=1 surfaces a critFail flag when the total fell short of difficulty. The widget rolls the Wild Die separately and reports its face + explode chain + critFail flag in the roll result. The narrator resolves the in-fiction effect of a critFail (OpenD6: 'subtract highest other die OR add complication' — GM-fiat). Don't read this as auto-resolution: widget surfaces a flag, narrator picks the consequence.

enabled.description: Master switch. When false, no Wild Die is designated and the rolled pool is a plain sum.

explodeFace.description: Face value that triggers an explode (re-roll, add the new face to the total; cascades while the new face also matches). Typically equal to `dieSize`.

critFailFace.description: Face value that signals a potential critical failure. The widget reports `critFail: true` in the roll result when the Wild Die landed on this face AND the rolled total fell short of difficulty. Narrator resolves.

explodeCap.description: Maximum chained explosions. 0 means uncapped (canon OpenD6). Set to N to cap at N chained explodes — useful for sanity-bounded pathological streaks.

description.description: Plain-language description of how the system resolves checks (e.g. 'Roll the GM-chosen formula; the narrator adjudicates outcomes from prose.'). The dice widget skips automatic resolution and surfaces a generic manual NdX widget the player can drive. Use this mode as a lossy-but-functional path for any system whose dice math doesn't fit the typed resolution modes — the schema validates, the widget runs, the narrator does the math.

noticeText.description: Optional banner text shown above the manual NdX widget (e.g. 'This ruleset uses narrative resolution — see the gm-agent prompt for adjudication rules.'). Defaults to a generic notice when omitted.

difficulties.description: Map of difficulty label -> threshold (DC for d20, success count for dice-pool, etc.).

attributes.description: Character attributes. Order in this array is the rendering order on the sheet within each group.

group.description: Optional grouping for sheet rendering (Physical / Social / Mental, etc.).

modifierFormula.description: Optional auto-calculated modifier for this attribute (D&D ability modifier, PF2e modifier, etc.). Use the magic token {Score} to reference this attribute's own raw value, plus standard {StatName} placeholders for cross-attribute references. Result is floored. Example: "({Score} - 10) / 2" for D&D 5e ability modifiers.

modifierName.description: Optional override for the modifier's name in formula references. Defaults to <name>_mod and <abbreviation>_mod (both auto-exposed). Use this only when neither default name fits.

aliases.description: Optional alternate names the LLM may use when emitting [mrrp-state: field="..."] tags for this attribute. The abbreviation is auto-recognized and does not need to be repeated here.

pipGranularity.description: Optional pip-granularity declaration for ND+P stat ratings (OpenD6, WEG Star Wars, Mini Six, D6 System SRD). When present, the sheet renders this attribute as 'N D+P' instead of just 'N', and the state-mutator accepts delta='+1pip' / '-1pip' / '+1die' separately. The advancement loop converts pips into dice when pip count reaches `pipsPerDie`. Pairs with `resolution.pipsField` on dice-pool-sum mode.

pipsPerDie.description: How many pips equal one die. OpenD6: 3.

pipsField.description: Name of the sheet sub-field carrying the pip count (defaults to 'pips' if omitted). The dice widget adds this resolved integer to dice-pool-sum rolls; the state-mutator writes pip deltas to this field.

saves.description: Optional saving throws (D&D, PF2e). Each save renders a row whose displayed value is auto-computed from the linked attribute's modifier plus the active proficiency tier. Saves use the same skillProficiency tiers as skills.

name.description: Display name for the save (e.g. "Strength Save", "Fortitude").

linkedAttribute.description: Name of the attribute whose modifier contributes to this save.

skills.description: Skills (or Abilities for Exalted). Order is the rendering order on the sheet.

linkedAttribute.description: Name of the attribute this skill defaults to. For Exalted, this is null/omitted because Storyteller picks the attribute per check.

aliases.description: Optional alternate names the LLM may use when emitting [mrrp-state: field="..."] tags for this skill.

pipGranularity.description: Optional pip-granularity declaration for ND+P stat ratings (OpenD6, WEG Star Wars, Mini Six). Same shape and semantics as attribute pipGranularity. Pairs with `resolution.pipsField` on dice-pool-sum mode. The advancement loop converts pips to dice when pip count reaches `pipsPerDie`.

pipsPerDie.description: How many pips equal one die. OpenD6: 3.

pipsField.description: Name of the sheet sub-field carrying the pip count (defaults to 'pips').

skillProficiency.description: Optional per-ruleset training/proficiency tier system. When present, each skill row shows a cycle button advancing through these tiers; the active tier's rollBonusFormula contributes to dice math via the existing formula evaluator. Omit the block entirely (Fate Core) for rulesets that don't use tiers.

tiers.description: Ordered list of training tiers. The first tier is the default for new characters unless `default` is set.

code.description: Short letter code shown on the cycle button (e.g. "U", "T", "E", "M", "L" for PF2e; "U", "C", "F" for Exalted).

label.description: Full tier name shown in the button tooltip (e.g. "Trained", "Caste").

rollBonusFormula.description: Plain formula contributed to dice math when this tier is active. Whitelist: digits, + - * / ( ) and {StatName} placeholders that resolve from attributes / skills / derivedStats. Use "0" for narrative-only tiers (Exalted Caste/Favored).

default.description: Tier code applied to skills with no saved tier (typically the lowest tier — "U" / Untrained / Unskilled). If omitted, the renderer falls back to the first tier in the array.

skillSpecialties.description: Optional named specialty support per skill. When enabled, each skill row gains a +S button to add a sub-row with a name and value; the dice widget surfaces applicable specialties as stackable checkboxes when rolling for the parent skill.

valueLabel.description: Human-readable label for what the value represents (e.g. "+1 die", "+1 to roll"). Shown beside the value stepper.

valueKind.description: How a checked specialty contributes to dice math: "dice" adds to the pool size, "value" adds to the modifier/skill input, "successes" adds auto-successes.

defaultValue.description: Default numeric value for a newly-added specialty (Exalted: 1).

header.description: Optional per-ruleset labels for the two free-text identity fields rendered in the sheet header (race/species/ancestry/exalt-type and class/caste). Defaults are "Race" and "Class" when this block is omitted.

raceLabel.description: Label for the first identity field (e.g. "Race", "Species", "Ancestry", "Exalt Type").

classLabel.description: Label for the second identity field (e.g. "Class", "Caste", "Concept", "Archetype").

backgrounds.description: Optional free-text dot-rated traits beyond attributes and skills (Exalted Backgrounds & Merits, World of Darkness Merits, D&D Feats, etc.). When enabled, the sheet renders a "backgrounds" section where the player adds named entries and steps each one's dot value within the configured range.

label.description: Section title shown in the UI (e.g. "Backgrounds & Merits").

min.description: Minimum dot value for a single background entry (default 0).

max.description: Maximum dot value for a single background entry (default 5).

default.description: Initial dot value for a newly-added background entry (default 0).

textOnly.description: When true, the section renders name-only rows (no dot value, no stepper). Use for systems whose entries are described, not rated — D&D 5e Feats use this. The user adds a lorebook entry per row to teach the agent the mechanics.

derivedStats.description: Stats computed from attributes/level/etc. Used for HUD readouts.

formula.description: Plain-language formula the GM evaluates (e.g. "Essence x 7 + 26" for peripheral motes).

default.description: Optional initial value for this derived stat on a blank sheet. When omitted, derived stats default to 0. Use this for stats whose semantic floor is not zero (D&D "Level" defaults to 1, etc.).

maxFormula.description: Optional auto-calculated max for renderAs=bar. Use {StatName} placeholders that resolve to the current attribute/skill/derived value (e.g. "{Essence} * 7 + 26"). Whitelist: digits, + - * / ( ) and {StatName} only.

valueFormula.description: Optional auto-calculated current value (autocalc). When present, the sheet displays the computed result and suppresses the manual stepper — the formula IS the override. Same {StatName} placeholder + arithmetic-only whitelist as maxFormula. Example: "({Dexterity} + {Melee}) / 2" for Exalted Defense (Parry).

tooltipFormula.description: Plan B v1 — token-substituted formula whose breakdown surfaces as the tooltip on the derived stat's value cell (Phase 5 step 5.4). Token convention matches skillBonusFormula: {StatName} resolves to the named attribute/skill/derived; {bonuses:<key>} sums equipment bonuses tagged with that key; {Level} resolves to character level; {tierBonus} resolves to the active proficiency tier's rollBonusFormula. The renderer evaluates this AND surfaces the substituted breakdown in a `title=` tooltip (e.g. 'Soak (Bashing): 7 = Stamina (4) + Armor (3)'). Distinct from `formula` (plain-language GM hint) and `valueFormula` (autocalc that suppresses the manual stepper). When both `tooltipFormula` and `valueFormula` are present, `valueFormula` drives the displayed value and `tooltipFormula` drives the tooltip; when only `tooltipFormula` is present, the renderer also uses it to compute the value.

rollFormula.description: Optional roll formula. When set on a single-roll-mode ruleset, the derived stat row shows a "roll" button that opens the dice widget pre-filled with 1d20 + this formula's value. Use for stats the player rolls directly (Initiative = "{Dexterity_mod}", Perception in PF2e = "{Wisdom_mod} + {tierBonus}").

aliases.description: Optional alternate names the LLM may use when emitting [mrrp-state: field="..."] tags. The state-mutator resolver matches case/punctuation-insensitively, so "Hit Points" listing aliases ["hp", "HP"] catches every common variant.

effects.description: Optional declarative effects this derived stat has on dice rolls when spent (Character Points, Fate Points, Force Points). When the player triggers a spend pre-roll, the dice widget reads these fields and applies the effect to the very-next-roll's pool/dice/modifier. The widget surfaces a 'Spend X?' prompt on roll-start when an enabled spend effect is present; the state-mutator emits the resource decrement as a delta. Multiple resources can each declare their own `effects` block — OpenD6 has Character Points (+1 die per spend) AND Fate Points (double total dice per spend) on separate derived stats.

onSpend.description: Effect applied to the next dice-pool-sum roll when this resource is spent pre-roll. Each sub-field is optional; declare what the resource does.

addDice.description: Number of extra dice added to the pool for the next roll (OpenD6 Character Point: 1).

doubleDice.description: When true, doubles the rolled pool size for the next roll (Star Wars D6 Force Point).

addPip.description: Number of pips added to the next roll's flat-modifier component.

penaltyKind.description: How the renderer interprets `penalty` for display and state-reminder text. 'flat' (default, back-compat) — penalty applies as a flat numeric subtraction to the relevant total (Exalted Wound Levels, WoD health-state penalties). 'dice' — penalty applies as a DICE subtraction (OpenD6 / WEG D6 / Mini Six Wound Levels: 'Wounded -1D' removes one die from every pool until healed). When 'dice', the renderer prefixes the cell text with 'D' (e.g. '-1D' instead of '-1') and the state-reminder agent emits 'minus N dice from every pool' instead of 'minus N to total'.

track.description: For renderAs=track: ordered cells (e.g. Exalted health levels).

damageTypes.description: Optional: declares typed damage on a renderAs=track derived stat. Each entry's id is the field name the state-mutator uses (field="<id>" delta="<+/-N>"). Higher severity stacks leftmost on the track. Renderer applies CSS modifier class .<prefix>-track__cell--<id> per typed cell.

states.description: Named UI states (Exalted anima banner, D&D conditions, etc.) the sheet can display as a current value.

diceTagFormat.description: How the GM agent should format the [dice: ...] tag the client parses. The extension's roller mirrors this exactly.

example.description: Concrete example output the GM should imitate (e.g. "[dice: 8d10 vs 7 -> 4 successes, 1 ten doubled]").

sheetSections.description: Order of sheet sections rendered by the extension. Recognized values: attributes, skills, saves, derived, states, conditions, backgrounds, inventory, charms, intimacies, abilities, notes. (Legacy field; new rulesets should prefer `sections.order` which supports the wider section vocabulary including identity / xp / resources / morality / spellbook.)

sections.description: Plan B v1 declarative section ordering and hiding. Backwards-compatible with `sheetSections`: when `sections.order` is present, the renderer uses it; otherwise it falls back to `sheetSections` or the hardcoded default order.

order.description: Explicit order of sections on the sheet. Sections not listed render at the end in extension-default order. Sections present in `hidden` are excluded regardless of their position here.

hidden.description: Section IDs (or 'derived:<name>' for individual derived stats) to hide for this ruleset. Examples: 'derived:Sorcery Circle' hides one derivedStats entry; 'disciplines' hides the entire disciplines section.

resources.description: Plan B v1 declarative list of expendable / tracked resources rendered in the Resources cluster at the top of the sheet body (above Attributes). Consumed by Phase 5 step 5.3. Open type system — each ruleset declares any shape it needs. When absent or empty, the Resources cluster does not render.

id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier. Used by the GM agent in [mrrp-state: resource:<id>=N] tags. NEVER rename — saved character resources orphan if the id changes.

label.description: Human-readable name shown on the Resources cluster (e.g. 'Blood Pool', 'Motes Personal', 'Hit Dice').

type.description: Render shape. 'bar' = horizontal fill bar with current/max. 'dice' = pool of dice (each clickable to spend; uses `die` size). 'counter' = numeric stepper. 'pool' = current/max with quick-buttons. 'custom' = registered named renderer via `rendererConfig.component`. 'state-banner' = surface a ruleset-declared state inline in the Resources cluster (uses `stateRef` to name the state) — click cycles through its declared values.

max.description: Token-substituted formula or static integer for max value. Tokens follow the same convention as skillBonusFormula — {StatName}, {bonuses:<key>}, {Level}, {tierBonus}. Examples: '{Stamina} * 2 + {Willpower}', or 10.

current.description: Default current value on a fresh sheet. Usually equal to max. Same token rules as `max`.

die.description: Die size for type=dice (e.g. d6, d8, d10). Required when type=dice; ignored otherwise.

group.description: Optional sub-group label (e.g. 'Spell Slots: Level 1'). Adjacent resources sharing a group render under a shared subheader.

color.description: Optional color override; default auto by fill percentage. 'bad' for blood/aggravated, 'accent' for willpower-style pools.

quickButtons.description: Optional one-click delta buttons rendered alongside the resource (e.g. [{label:'Spend 1', delta:-1}, {label:'Refresh', delta:'max'}]).

delta.description: Integer delta applied on click, or the magic string 'max' to refill to current max.

rendererConfig.description: Free-form blob for type=custom. Consumed by a registered named renderer (e.g. {component:'v20-health-track', levels:[...], damageTypes:[...]}). The extension knows no custom components by default — the ruleset must reference a component name the extension JS has registered.

stateName.description: Optional legacy-state binding. When set, the resource reads/writes state.sheet.derived[stateName] instead of the new state.sheet.resources[id].current — keeping the value in the same store the legacy state-mutator chat-tag parser writes to (e.g. [mrrp-state: field="Personal Motes" delta=-3]). Used to surface LLM mutations in the Resources cluster automatically and to inherit the persistence path the 'derived' mergeSheet whitelist already supports. Resources without a legacy counterpart (e.g. sorcerous-motes) omit this and use the new resources store directly.

stateRef.description: For type='state-banner': the name of a state declared in this ruleset's states[] array (e.g. 'Anima Banner'). The renderer reads the state's `values` for its cycle/dropdown and reads/writes state.sheet.states[stateRef] for the current value. List the state-banner's parent state in `sections.hidden` as 'state:<Name>' to avoid double-rendering.

commitmentPool.description: When set (and the ruleset's commitmentModel is 'mote'), reduces this resource's effective max by the live sum of moteCommitment from equipped items whose `motePool` matches this value. Typical: 'Personal' or 'Peripheral' for Exalted motes. The renderer's commitment reconciler also adjusts the saved spendable on every commit / uncommit / slot-displacement, so equipping a charm-bound artifact locks the motes from the spendable pool and unequipping releases them — through the same code path.

morality.description: Plan B v1 optional morality / path / virtue section. Vampire-flavored but generic enough for any honor/sanity/corruption mechanic (CoC Sanity, L5R Honor, etc.). When present, the sheet renders a Morality section per the `sections.order` placement.

rating.description: The main morality scalar (e.g. Humanity 0-10, Path Rating 0-10, Sanity 0-99).

label.description: Display name for the rating (e.g. 'Path Rating', 'Sanity', 'Honor').

paths.description: Selectable paths/codes when the rating's flavor varies (e.g. Humanity, Honorable Accord, Caine, Beast, Night for Vampire). Renders as a dropdown beside the rating. Omit entirely for systems with a single fixed code (Sanity).

id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier for the path. NEVER rename.

loreRef.description: Optional lorebook entry key the GM agent should consult for hierarchy-of-sins / path mechanics.

virtues.description: Optional virtue ratings alongside the main rating. Supports paired-choice virtues (Conscience/Conviction, Self-Control/Instinct) via the `options` field, which renders the row as a dropdown of the two labels plus the numeric value.

options.description: When present, render the virtue as a dropdown of these labels with the chosen one's value alongside (e.g. [{id:'conscience',label:'Conscience'},{id:'conviction',label:'Conviction'}]).

conditions.description: Optional status condition definitions. When the ruleset declares a list, the sheet renders a Conditions section with an add dropdown of these names plus a free-text option. Each condition can declare which roll categories (attack / save / skill) it imposes disadvantage or grants advantage on; the quickRoll* helpers consult those when opening the dice widget so the player doesn't have to remember every condition's effect.

description.description: One-line rules summary, surfaced as a tooltip on the condition row and embedded in the agent prompt.

imposesDisadvantageOn.description: Roll categories on which this condition imposes disadvantage when active. The dice widget auto-arms disadvantage on the matching category.

grantsAdvantageOn.description: Roll categories on which this condition grants advantage when active.

lorebookKeys.description: Suggested lorebook trigger keywords the user should ensure are present in the bundled lorebook.json.

equipmentSlots.description: Optional advisory list of equipment slot names the inventory UI will autocomplete against (e.g. ["weapon", "offhand", "armor"]). Items may still use slots not in this list — the loader does not enforce membership.

equipmentBonusTargets.description: Optional advisory list of stat names that authors are encouraged to use as bonus targets (e.g. "Melee", "Defense (Parry)", "Soak (Bashing)"). Targets are matched by exact string against attributes, skills, or derivedStats names; the loader does not enforce membership.

abilities.description: Optional powers/abilities/charms slot. Drives a per-character spellbook flyout panel that lets players record system-specific powers (D&D spells, Exalted Charms, Fate stunts, etc.) and auto-creates a chat-scoped lorebook entry per ability so the GM agent reads the description when the keyword fires. The optional `groups` field (Plan B v1) groups the ruleset's top-level skills into named buckets for sheet rendering (V20 Talents/Skills/Knowledges).

label.description: System-appropriate label shown on the main sheet row and on the flyout header (e.g. "Spellbook", "Charms", "Stunts", "Disciplines").

groups.description: Plan B v1 — optional groups that bucket the ruleset's `skills[]` entries under named subheaders on the sheet (V20 Talents/Skills/Knowledges; could be used for D&D-by-attribute grouping). Each group lists the skill names that belong to it; skills not listed in any group render under an implicit 'Other' bucket. Distinct from `categories` (which groups recorded abilities/spells in the flyout).

label.description: Subheader label shown above the group's skills on the sheet.

members.description: Names of `skills[]` entries that belong to this group. Names must match the corresponding `skills[].name` exactly.

categories.description: Buckets the abilities organize into. Spell levels for d20, ability buckets for Exalted, a single "Stunts" entry for Fate, etc.

id.description: Stable identifier (kebab-case). NEVER rename — abilities saved against a categoryId orphan if the id changes.

defaultCollapsed.description: If true (default), the category renders collapsed on first open and the count badge tells the player how many abilities are inside.

roundCounters.description: Optional ephemeral per-round counters that the framework auto-resets on a 'round ended' signal from the combat-adjudicator agent. Use for OpenD6's multi-action penalty (declare N actions → every roll that round takes -(N-1) dice), Mini Six rate-of-fire penalties, or any per-round-only modifier that should NOT persist as a sticky state. The schema declares the counter; v0.2.2 does NOT auto-wire the dice-widget penalty application (that's downstream UX work — Task #5). Authors using v0.2.2 should document the workaround in their gm-agent.md and rely on the narrator to apply the penalty.

name.description: Display name shown on the combat panel (e.g. 'Action Penalty').

max.description: Optional cap on the counter's value.

resetOn.description: When the framework auto-zeroes this counter. 'next-round' clears on the combat-adjudicator's 'round ended' signal. 'end-of-encounter' clears when combat exits.

penaltyFormula.description: Optional formula describing how this counter penalizes rolls (e.g. '-(N - 1) dice' for OpenD6's multi-action penalty). Reference for the gm-agent / dice widget; v0.2.2 surfaces this as text only — auto-apply to roll pools deferred to Task #5.
